key_highlights: Global markets are in a broad sell-off driven by geopolitical tensions from the Iran war and disappointing outcomes from Trump's China trip, with bond yields spiking and risk assets under pressure.
Geopolitical risks dominate: record drone attack on Moscow, Iran war escalation, and a global inventory race are fueling uncertainty and oil price volatility.
Tech sector shows divergence: AI-related stocks like Cerebras soar 90% in IPO, but bond selloff threatens to derail the AI stock frenzy; retail euphoria drives record AI trades.
Central bank policy remains a key theme: ECB signals modest rate hike, Trump Fed nominees oppose keeping Powell as temporary chair, and global bond rout catches up with Wall Street.
Defensive plays and energy stocks gain favor amid turmoil: Venture Global soars on Iran peace uncertainties, SolarEdge hits 2-year high, and dividend aristocrats under $100 are highlighted.
Consumer and regulatory news: Amazon sued over tariff refunds, Starbucks lays off 300 corporate employees, and subscription fatigue is rising.
India tightens silver import rules to defend rupee, while China's market watchdog supports private sector, signaling policy divergence.
SpaceX plans IPO filing as soon as Wednesday, and AI chipmaker Cerebras has year's biggest IPO, highlighting continued investor appetite for high-growth tech.
Labor disruptions: NY commuter rail strike strands 300,000 daily riders, adding to economic friction.
Health emergency: WHO declares Congo Ebola outbreak a global health emergency, with a rare strain and no vaccine.

sectors.trend: Divergent performance: AI and software stocks like Cerebras, UiPath, Freshworks, Figma, and Atlassian show strong gains, but broader tech is under pressure from rising bond yields and geopolitical uncertainty. Retail euphoria is fueling record AI trades, but bond selloff threatens to knock the AI stock frenzy off course.
sectors.implications: Investors should differentiate between high-quality AI plays with strong fundamentals and speculative names. Rising rates could compress valuations for high-growth tech, making selective positioning critical.

sectors.trend: Oil prices are volatile due to Iran war uncertainties, global inventory race, and geopolitical tensions. Venture Global soars 9% on peace uncertainties, while SolarEdge hits 2-year high on strong Q2 hopes. Winners and losers of oil's new world order are emerging.
sectors.implications: Energy sector offers both opportunities and risks. Companies with exposure to LNG and renewable energy may benefit from supply disruptions and policy shifts. Investors should hedge against oil price spikes.

sectors.trend: Bond selloff is catching up with Wall Street's risk rally. Public BDCs are pricing in most pain since Covid. JPMorgan faces a messy sexual-assault lawsuit complicated by AI. Berkshire boosted Alphabet stake, bought Delta Air, sold Visa/Mastercard. Pershing Square bought Microsoft, exited Hilton, slashed Alphabet.
sectors.implications: Rising yields benefit banks' net interest margins but hurt bond portfolios. Investors should favor well-capitalized banks and BDCs with strong credit quality. Hedge fund positioning shifts suggest caution on consumer exposure.

sectors.trend: Starbucks lays off 300 corporate employees as part of turnaround strategy. Callaway stock soars 150% but one fund still bought 1.1 million shares. Amazon sued over tariff refunds. Subscription fatigue is alienating consumers. IKEA shopper tricks highlight value-seeking behavior.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending is under pressure from inflation and tariff uncertainty. Companies with strong brand loyalty and cost-cutting measures may outperform. Value-oriented retail and experiences could gain share.

sectors.trend: HELOC and home equity loan rates remain low but poised to rise. Zillow CEO prepares for next revolution in real estate. Australia announces tax changes to fix broken housing market. Retrofitting adds €12.2k to value of Dublin houses.
sectors.implications: Rising mortgage rates and bond yields could pressure housing affordability. However, home equity remains strong, and renovation/retrofitting trends offer opportunities. Investors should monitor rate-sensitive REITs.

sectors.trend: DexCom posts upbeat 2026, climbs 6.6%. WHO declares Congo Ebola outbreak a global health emergency with rare strain and no vaccine. Peptides mentioned in lifestyle headlines.
sectors.implications: Healthcare remains a defensive play. Diabetes and medical device companies like DexCom show strong growth. Infectious disease outbreaks could boost vaccine and diagnostic stocks.

sectors.trend: Global inventory race intensifies due to Iran war. Vietnam-bound supertanker halted by US Navy resumes journey. Malaysia unveils measures to help airlines hit by Iran war. NY commuter rail strike strands 300,000 daily riders.
sectors.implications: Supply chain disruptions and geopolitical tensions are boosting demand for logistics, shipping, and defense. Infrastructure spending may increase. Labor strikes add to operational risks.

key_themes: Geopolitical turmoil: Iran war, drone attack on Moscow, China summit disappointment, Taiwan ambiguity, Russia-China cooperation.
Global bond selloff: Rising yields, oil price spike, risk-off sentiment, bond rout catching up with Wall Street.
AI and tech divergence: AI stocks surge (Cerebras, UiPath, Freshworks, Figma) but bond selloff threatens sustainability; retail euphoria drives record AI trades.
Central bank policy uncertainty: ECB modest hike, Trump Fed nominees oppose Powell, global monetary policy divergence.
Inflation and tariff concerns: Amazon sued over tariff refunds, rising oil prices, global inventory race, India silver import rules.
Defensive rotation: Dividend aristocrats, high-yield savings, CDs, money market accounts gain attention.
Space and defense: SpaceX IPO filing, Google-SpaceX orbital data centers, defense stocks benefit from geopolitical tensions.
Labor and social unrest: NY commuter rail strike, Starbucks layoffs, AP layoffs, subscription fatigue.
Health emergency: Congo Ebola outbreak declared global health emergency.
Regulatory and legal: JPMorgan AI lawsuit, Chick-fil-A religious discrimination suit, OpenAI trial, China private sector support.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase allocation to defensive sectors (energy, healthcare, utilities), hedge with gold or commodities, reduce exposure to emerging markets directly affected.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Global bond selloff and rising yields
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in fixed income, favor floating-rate bonds, increase cash and short-term treasuries, consider TIPS for inflation protection.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil price spike and supply disruptions
risk_assessment.mitigation: Overweight energy stocks, add commodity exposure, hedge with oil futures or ETFs, reduce exposure to airlines and transport.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI stock bubble and retail euphoria
risk_assessment.mitigation: Take profits on speculative AI names, focus on companies with strong fundamentals and earnings, use options to hedge downside.
Diversify across currencies, favor central banks with clear communication, monitor inflation data closely.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Labor strikes and supply chain disruptions
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid stocks directly impacted by strikes (e.g., commuter rail), diversify supply chains, consider onshoring beneficiaries.
Monitor for broader spread, consider vaccine and healthcare stocks as hedges, avoid travel to affected regions.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and legal risks (tariffs, AI lawsuits, discrimination)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid companies with significant litigation exposure, favor companies with strong compliance, monitor tariff policy changes.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy high-quality AI and software stocks with strong earnings momentum (e.g., Cerebras, UiPath, Freshworks, Figma, Atlassian) on pullbacks, but take profits on speculative names.
opportunities.rationale: AI remains a secular growth theme, but rising yields and geopolitical risks create volatility. Focus on companies with solid fundamentals, revenue beats, and clear AI monetization.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to energy and defense stocks benefiting from geopolitical tensions (e.g., Venture Global, SolarEdge, defense contractors).
opportunities.rationale: Iran war uncertainties and global inventory race support oil prices and energy infrastructure. Defense spending is likely to increase. SolarEdge's new product launch and strong Q2 hopes add upside.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in dividend aristocrats and defensive stocks under $100 for long-term income and stability.
opportunities.rationale: In a volatile market with rising yields, high-quality dividend payers offer income and downside protection. Companies with consistent dividend growth are attractive.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider high-yield savings accounts, CDs, and money market accounts offering up to 4.1% APY for cash reserves.
opportunities.rationale: Rising interest rates make cash equivalents attractive for short-term liquidity and safety. Lock in rates before potential further hikes.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Berkshire Hathaway and Pershing Square holdings (Alphabet, Microsoft, Delta Air) on dips, following insider buying patterns.
opportunities.rationale: Berkshire and Pershing Square have strong track records. Their Q1 portfolio moves signal confidence in Alphabet, Microsoft, and Delta Air, while reducing consumer exposure.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-growth tech and speculative AI stocks; rotate into value, energy, and healthcare.
defensive_moves.rationale: Bond selloff and geopolitical risks are compressing valuations for high-growth names. Defensive sectors offer better risk-adjusted returns.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation and short-term treasuries to 15-20% of portfolio.
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields and market uncertainty warrant higher liquidity. Cash provides optionality to buy dips and hedge against further sell-offs.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against oil price spikes using oil futures or ETFs (e.g., USO, XLE).
defensive_moves.rationale: Iran war and global inventory race could push oil prices higher, impacting transportation and consumer stocks. Energy exposure provides a natural hedge.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid stocks with high exposure to labor strikes, tariff disputes, or regulatory lawsuits (e.g., Amazon, JPMorgan, Chick-fil-A operator).
defensive_moves.rationale: Legal and operational risks can lead to significant downside. Focus on companies with clean regulatory records and stable labor relations.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor and potentially reduce exposure to emerging markets, particularly India and China, given policy uncertainty and geopolitical risks.
defensive_moves.rationale: India missed out on AI and its market darling status may be ending. China's summit produced few deals, and Taiwan ambiguity adds risk. Silver import rules and private sector support are mixed signals.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Negative to cautious. Geopolitical tensions (Iran war, Russia-Ukraine, China-Taiwan) and global bond selloff will likely keep markets volatile. Oil prices may spike further, pressuring equities. AI stocks could see profit-taking after euphoric runs. Defensive sectors and cash will outperform. Central bank actions (ECB, Fed) will be key. Labor strikes and health emergencies add to uncertainty.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Neutral with upside potential. If geopolitical tensions de-escalate and inflation moderates, markets could recover. AI and tech remain long-term growth drivers. Energy transition and infrastructure spending offer opportunities. However, structural risks from rising rates, deglobalization, and demographic shifts persist. Diversification and quality will be rewarded.
key_catalysts: Iran war developments: peace negotiations or escalation.
Trump's China trip outcomes: trade deals or further tensions.
Central bank meetings: ECB rate decision, Fed guidance, Trump Fed nominees.
Oil price trajectory: impact on inflation and consumer spending.
AI earnings season: Cerebras, UiPath, Freshworks, Figma results and guidance.
SpaceX IPO filing: expected as soon as Wednesday, could boost space and tech sentiment.
Congo Ebola outbreak: potential for wider spread or containment.
US labor market data: impact on Fed policy and consumer confidence.
Global inventory race: supply chain adjustments and trade flows.
Regulatory developments: Amazon tariff lawsuit, JPMorgan AI case, OpenAI trial.
watch_list: Central bank speeches and policy statements
AI and tech stock valuations and earnings
Currency markets (USD, EUR, JPY, INR, CNY)
Labor strike developments (NY commuter rail, other sectors)
Consumer sentiment and retail sales data
Corporate earnings and guidance from key sectors
